description: Apple's '1984' commercial, aired during Super Bowl XVIII, introduced the Macintosh with an Orwellian theme. The ad faced internal resistance from the board but became a watershed moment for cinematic marketing.

key_events.description: Apple Board of Directors expresses deep skepticism regarding the ad's lack of product shots.
Super Bowl XVIII air date; immediate media frenzy and critical acclaim.
Post-game news analysis highlights the ad's impact on brand identity perception.
Sales data confirms massive consumer interest directly linked to commercial recall.

calibration_notes: Models disruptive marketing adoption curves vs. traditional product-focused advertising.
